pr.title: lb: match fn_80026650 (100%), improve fn_800268B4 and lb_8000FD48
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: PR #2509 made focused matching improvements in the lb library area: fn_80026650 in lbaudio_ax.c reached 100%, fn_800268B4 improved slightly, and lb_8000FD48 in lb_00F9.c improved by factoring repeated free-list pop logic into a static inline helper. The PR body reports local configure/ninja and per-function checkdiff verification; the bot report corroborates one new match and two unmatched-item improvements.

key_files.role: Matched/improved lb audio functions by using direct named extern arrays and a pointer-increment loop form in fn_80026650 and fn_800268B4.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbaudio_ax.c hunks around fn_80026650 and fn_800268B4

key_files.role: Improved lb_8000FD48 by extracting repeated cur_data free-list pop logic into static inline popDynamicsData.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lb_00F9.c hunk adding popDynamicsData and replacing duplicated pop blocks

what_changed: In src/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.c, fn_80026650 changed arr_38a4 and arr_3984 initialization from lbl_80433710.x194/x274 to direct named externs lbl_804338A4 and lbl_80433984.

In src/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.c, fn_800268B4 made the same direct named-extern swap, changed the loop condition from i > 0 to i != 0, replaced indexed accesses with dereferenced moving pointers, and increments arr_38a4, arr_3984, and arr_4e4 each iteration.

In src/melee/lb/lb_00F9.c, repeated code that popped one DynamicsData node from cur_data was extracted into static inline popDynamicsData, then reused for both first and subsequent list entries in lb_8000FD48.
The PR body states fn_80026650 went from 78.6% baseline to 100%, fn_800268B4 improved from 88.5% to 88.7%, and lb_8000FD48 improved from 93.7% to 94.2%.
The automated decomp-dev bot report corroborates fn_80026650 as one new 100.00% match and reports lb_8000FD48 improving 93.66% to 94.19% and fn_800268B4 improving 88.47% to 88.71%.
smart_moves: Used direct named externs lbl_804338A4 and lbl_80433984 instead of reaching those arrays through the broader lbl_80433710 struct anchor, which the PR body says produced the fn_80026650 match and contributed to fn_800268B4 improvement.
Converted fn_800268B4 to a pointer-increment traversal over the flag and offset arrays; the PR body specifically ties this to a CTR-loop prologue using bdnz/mtctr matching the expected layout.

Factored duplicated free-list pop logic into a static inline helper popDynamicsData, reducing duplicated generated-looking blocks while preserving the cur_data update and entry->next clearing behavior.
Kept the lb_8000FD48 helper local and static inline, matching the PR body's stated intent to mirror an existing inlineA0 pattern rather than introducing a wider API surface.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function currently accesses array regions through a larger struct anchor, try direct named externs for the actual arrays if symbol evidence exists; in this PR that direct access was associated with fn_80026650 reaching 100% and fn_800268B4 improving.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: named externs lbl_804338A4/lbl_80433984 directly instead of lbl_80433710 struct anchor
text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report for fn_80026650 and fn_800268B4

decomp_lessons.lesson: For repeated free-list pop blocks, a small static inline helper can both express the authored operation and improve matching when it mirrors nearby/local inline patterns.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: popDynamicsData helper extracts repeated cur_data pop code
human_pr_text: helper mirrors existing inlineA0 pattern and improves lb_8000FD48

decomp_lessons.lesson: For fixed-count array scans, pointer-increment loop forms with end/count conditions like i != 0 can be worth testing when indexed forms do not produce the expected CTR loop shape.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_800268B4 changes from arr[i] indexing to *arr with post-iteration pointer increments
human_pr_text: pointer-increment loop form produced bdnz/mtctr CTR-loop matching expected layout

naming_conventions.name: popDynamicsData
naming_conventions.lesson: Helper name describes the free-list operation on DynamicsData and is local to lb_00F9.c.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static inline struct DynamicsData* popDynamicsData(void)
human_pr_text: Refactor lb_8000FD48 free-list pop into popDynamicsData

naming_conventions.name: lbl_804338A4 / lbl_80433984
naming_conventions.lesson: PR evidence favors using these direct existing symbol names over a broader struct-anchor access for the touched lbaudio_ax functions; it does not prove semantic names for the arrays.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: using named externs directly instead of lbl_80433710 struct anchor
raw_diff: replacement of lbl_80433710.x194/x274 with lbl_804338A4/lbl_80433984

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Direct extern array access instead of struct-anchor field access
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: fn_80026650 and fn_800268B4 initialize arr_38a4/arr_3984 from lbl_804338A4/lbl_80433984 rather than lbl_80433710.x194/x274.
fn_800268B4 uses *arr_38a4, *arr_3984, and (*arr_4e4)[0], increments the pointers each iteration, and uses i != 0. The PR body reports this made the prologue use bdnz/mtctr CTR-loop layout.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_800268B4 loop and pointer increments

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Static inline extraction of repeated list-pop logic
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: lb_8000FD48 replaces two duplicated cur_data pop sequences with popDynamicsData(), which returns NULL for empty list, advances cur_data, clears entry->next, and returns the popped entry.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: free-list pop inline mirroring inlineA0

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: PR body reports python configure.py && ninja was green and each touched function was rechecked individually with tools/checkdiff.py; bot report corroborates score changes but not the local build command.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code 67.17% (+0.01%, +352 bytes), one new match for main/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax fn_80026650 at 100.00%, and two improvements: lb_8000FD48 93.66% to 94.19%, fn_800268B4 88.47% to 88.71%.
No human review comments were present in the loaded PR slice.

notes: The core code changes and score deltas are well supported by the diff, PR body, and bot report.
The PR body's local build and per-function checkdiff claims are author-provided; the bot report corroborates score movement but not the exact local configure/ninja command.
No human review comments are available, so reviewer intent or requested changes should not be inferred.
The body says popDynamicsData mirrors an existing inlineA0 pattern, but the loaded diff does not show inlineA0 itself; treat that as PR-author context rather than independently verified local style.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Try direct named extern arrays before broad struct-anchor access when symbols identify the data
accepted_candidate_records.text: In lb audio matching, replacing lbl_80433710.x194/x274 accesses with direct named externs lbl_804338A4/lbl_80433984 was associated with fn_80026650 reaching 100% and fn_800268B4 improving. Keep semantic claims limited to the evidence; this PR supports the access-shape tactic, not a broader rename.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: named externs directly instead of struct anchor

accepted_candidate_records.title: Pointer-increment loops can recover CTR-loop shape
accepted_candidate_records.text: For fn_800268B4, changing indexed array access to moving pointers with a fixed countdown loop was reported to produce bdnz/mtctr CTR-loop layout and a small checkdiff improvement.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: pointer-increment loop form and bdnz/mtctr note
raw_diff: pointer dereference and post-iteration increments

accepted_candidate_records.title: Extract repeated free-list pop code into a local static inline when it matches local pattern
accepted_candidate_records.text: In lb_8000FD48, duplicated cur_data pop logic was replaced with static inline popDynamicsData, improving the function's reported match percent. This aligns with natural-loop/authored-source cleanup principles but remains scoped to helper extraction for repeated list-pop idioms.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: mirrors existing inlineA0 pattern and improves score

rejection_notes: Do not promote lbl_804338A4 or lbl_80433984 to semantic names from this PR alone; evidence only supports using the existing named externs directly.
Do not generalize that all struct-anchor field accesses in lbaudio_ax should be replaced by direct externs; this PR only covers the touched arrays/functions.
Do not claim fn_800268B4 or lb_8000FD48 matched 100%; evidence only reports improvements to about 88.7% and 94.2%.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; loaded slice has no review comments.
